page: Amazon Web Services r5n.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. The instance provides 16 vCPUs across 8 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per vCPU. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance type, and it features a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core tests, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance and single-threaded gzip compression, fall into the weak category. However, multi-core operations, including stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, consistently achieve average tier results. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks also register in the average range, supporting workloads that require steady data throughput.

Given its high memory density and average multi-core performance, the r5n.4xlarge is designed for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high single-core compute speeds. It is suitable for database operations, caching layers like Redis, and static web serving, where its average database and Redis SET benchmark results align with operational requirements. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio on dedicated hardware, making it a viable option for memory-bound applications that can tolerate moderate CPU performance.
description: Amazon Web Services r5n.4xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory, running on an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor at 3.1 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides a dedicated CPU allocation with 8 physical cores and a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data reveals a performance tradeoff: single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression tasks are weak, falling into the bottom 25%, whereas multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations consistently rank in the average tier. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy applications that do not demand high single-core processing speeds. The r5n.4xlarge is suited for memory-intensive workloads such as database operations, Redis caching, and static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5n.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 3.1 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, delivering average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance for database workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized instance featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and average multi-core performance for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.

Dedicated CPU allocation with 8 physical cores running at 3.1 GHz
Baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps with no local storage
Average multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmark performance

Suited for database operations, Redis caching, and static web serving
